title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Some spinosaurs cried salty tears to thrive in brackish waters
items.description: Fossil evidence suggests some predatory dinosaurs could expel salt from their bloodstream like modern birds and crocodiles do

items.title: Surveys capture the pulsing of mantle plumes—a potential cause of mass extinctions
items.description: Seismic imaging and drilling cruises bolster a controversial explanation for ancient surges in warming

items.title: Lawmakers propose banning all U.S.-Chinese research collaborations
items.description: Critics say bill would weaken U.S. science by cutting flows of ideas and talent

items.title: Wikipedia’s gender gap has flipped for one group of scientists
items.description: A new study finds women biology faculty are now more likely than men to have a biography on the popular website

items.title: Outdoor lights may be making mosquito season longer
items.description: Artificial light at night delays winter “shutdown” of mosquitoes carrying disease

items.title: Can fast, nimble clinical trials deliver a drug for the new Ebola outbreak?
items.description: Past outbreaks spawned clever strategies for testing antivirals and antibodies, but researchers will still face major challenges on the ground

items.title: Meet the microbiologist and science advocate who’s headed to Congress
items.description: Jasmine Clark led a March for Science in 2017. She just won a Georgia primary for a safe Democratic House seat

items.title: It’s official: Europe physicists plan to build 91-kilometer particle collider
items.description: The $19 billion Future Circular Collider would build on results from CERN’s Large Hadron Collider, currently the world’s largest

items.title: U.S. agencies aren’t ready for the rising cost of making research papers free, report warns
items.description: Science agencies could soon face nearly $1 billion publishing bill, Congressional analysts find

items.title: Research groups want Senate to vet Trump’s nominee for NSF head
items.description: A hearing would air questions about James O’Neill’s qualifications to lead the agency
